name: API Glass Dashboard
summary: A reusable premium enterprise dashboard design system with layered glass surfaces, soft gradients, rounded geometry, restrained branding, and first-class light/dark themes.

intent.goal: Make multiple apps feel like the same product family without copying full screens.
success_criteria: New apps can recreate the same visual language from tokens and shared components.
Codex can read this file and produce UI consistent with the system.
Visual refactors can be separated from business-logic changes.
The system can evolve with versioning instead of ad hoc per-project styling.
design_principles: Prefer tokens and shared primitives over one-off styling.
Favor layered translucent surfaces over flat boxes.
Use accent color sparingly for emphasis and status, not as a default fill.
Keep layouts clean, spacious, and dashboard-oriented.
Preserve a premium enterprise tone instead of playful consumer styling.
Support dark mode as a first-class mode, not a late-stage inversion.
Branding, logo usage, and layout tone are part of the system, not separate concerns.

usage_rules: Use the full logo by default in hero or primary brand areas.
Use the compact logo only when the layout is tight or when a reduced brand mark is needed.
Use the light-theme logo on light backgrounds.
Use the dark-theme logo variant on dark surfaces.
Do not recolor logo assets ad hoc in product UI.
Do not stretch the logo disproportionately.
Prefer placing the logo inside premium surface areas with ample whitespace.

placement_rules: Place the logo near the top-left of hero or admin headers.
Keep clear space around the logo equal to at least the height of the letter A in the mark.
Do not crowd the logo with dense controls or body copy.

implementation_portability.tailwind_projects: Use CSS variables for tokens and thin wrapper components for repeated variants.
implementation_portability.non_tailwind_projects: Preserve token semantics, component roles, spacing, and hierarchy in the local styling system.

style_rules: Use the UI font for all interface text.
Reserve mono for technical or dense structured values.
Use large, bold, low-line-height headings in hero sections.
Use muted secondary copy instead of reducing layout density.

rules: Default to generous internal spacing in cards and panels.
Prefer 1rem to 1.5rem gaps for related controls.
Use 2rem or more between major page sections.
Default borders should be subtle and token-driven.
Dashed borders are appropriate for upload zones and optional drop targets.
Favor opacity, transform, border-color, box-shadow, and background-color changes.
Reserve high stacking values for tooltips and floating interactive elements.

requirements: Interactive elements must have visible focus states.
Color alone must not carry critical meaning.
Support keyboard interaction for toggles, disclosure panels, file upload affordances, and form controls.
Use semantic HTML whenever possible before introducing ARIA.
Preserve readable contrast in both light and dark themes.

component_notes.button: Buttons must expose disabled state and preserve accessible labels.
component_notes.theme_toggle: Theme switches should expose switch semantics and current state.
component_notes.collapsible_sections: Disclosure controls should expose aria-expanded and aria-controls.
component_notes.forms: Inputs need labels or equivalent accessible names.

usage_rules: Use panelStrong for hero or high-importance containers.
Use panel for standard content sections.
Use panelSoft for nested control areas.
Use brand for inset cards, summaries, and supporting content.
Use primary for the main action in a section.
Use secondary for navigation, toggles, and low-emphasis actions.
Use danger for reset or destructive flows only.
Use rounded fields with soft surfaces and subtle focus rings.
Prefer comfortable padding over compact density.
Keep placeholder and helper text muted.
Use badges for compact labels, not as primary calls to action.
Keep them uppercase with generous letter spacing.

switch.notes: Theme toggle provides a switch-style interaction pattern.

tooltip.notes: Inline tooltip pattern exists in coverage metric card.

table.notes: Data tables exist as app-specific components; formal generic primitive still needed.

empty_state.notes: Pattern exists in page-level usage but not as a standalone generic component.

loader.notes: Loading surfaces exist but no shared spinner/loading primitive yet.

usage_rules: Use for section intros with optional right-side action or metadata.
Keep eyebrow short and uppercase.
Use for dataset summaries, compact stats, or support content.
Place inside larger sections, not as the page shell.

rules: Keep logo and title near the top-left.
Support one or more actions on the right or below the intro copy.

metric_card.notes: Represented by SummaryCard and related summary surfaces.

upload_dropzone.notes: App-specific but reusable as a branded upload pattern.

headings: Headings should be short and direct.
Use eyebrow plus title when section context matters.
body_copy: Use muted explanatory text under major titles.
Avoid overly dense paragraphs inside dashboards.
labels: Use explicit field labels for forms.
Use uppercase badges and eyebrows sparingly.
actions: Each section should have one clear primary action at most.

alignment: Prefer grid layouts for metrics and supporting sections.
Keep hero content readable with controlled max widths.
density: Prefer breathable spacing over compact enterprise density.
Use nested soft panels before adding more border complexity.

requirements: Every core surface and control must have light and dark behavior.
Dark mode should keep the premium glass feel rather than becoming flat charcoal UI.
Logo variants must respect the active theme.

dos: Use gradients and atmospheric background effects subtly.
Use dark mode as a first-class theme, not an afterthought.
Use muted copy for explanatory text.
Use green accent for emphasis and positive directional cues.
Treat logo usage as part of the brand system, not a decorative afterthought.
donts: Do not default to flat white cards everywhere.
Do not overuse the accent green as a full-page dominant color.
Do not use sharp corners unless there is a specific reason.
Do not introduce playful or overly consumer-style visuals.
Do not mix unrelated visual styles across pages.
Do not improvise new logo colors or distort the logo mark.

governance.source_of_truth: This repository is currently the source of truth for the system.
change_policy: Token changes should be made before page-level overrides.
New components should be added to the design layer before being duplicated in app screens.
Breaking visual changes should increment the system version.
Downstream apps should adopt design-system updates intentionally, not by drift.

rules: Patch: wording or documentation changes with no visual contract changes.
Major: breaking token renames, behavior changes, or component API shifts.

short_term: Add missing primitive components like textarea, checkbox, modal, toast, tabs, and tooltip.
Convert remaining app-specific patterns into more generic reusable components.
Expand the design-system showcase page with states and usage examples.
medium_term: Extract design primitives into a shared package or copyable design folder.
Create screenshot references for key patterns.
Apply the same system to additional apps to validate portability.
long_term: Publish the system as an internal package or starter template.
Introduce automated visual regression tests for core components.

how_to_use: Load this file before generating UI in another project.
Treat tokens and usage rules as constraints, not optional suggestions.
Prefer implementing primitives first, then composing screens from them.
If the new project uses Tailwind, map these rules to utility classes and CSS variables.
If the new project does not use Tailwind, preserve the tokens, component roles, and visual rules in the local styling system.
Preserve dark mode, branding, and logo rules together with colors and surfaces.
codex_guidance.important_note: This file describes the design system. It does not replace the actual CSS and component implementation.
